description: Cluster-bootstrap CIs (B=10000, resampling the n<=10 distinct b*c products with replacement) for the product-keyed-vs-magnitude dissociation of a-0013, over the saved rule_vs_lookup_exp_g single-layer JSONs. CPU/stdlib-only, zero new forward passes. Reports 90% percentile CIs for the raw product correlation, partial(product|max-operand), partial(max-operand|product), and their difference (>0 => product-keying beats generic operand magnitude).
motivation: a-0013's partial-correlation dissociation (DeepSeek L23 partial(prod|max)=+0.69 vs partial(max|prod)=-0.26) was flagged 'indicative, not decisive' on n<=10 products with no uncertainty quantification. Bootstrap CIs say which claim survives resampling and which bands are genuinely indeterminate.
formal_results: DeepSeek-Math-7B L23 (the one genuinely staged late band): difference partial(prod|max)-partial(max|prod) point=+0.95, 90% CI [+0.50,+1.50], P(diff>0)=1.00 across 10000 replicates -> product-keying robustly beats max-operand control. The partial(prod|max) effect-size point is +0.69 but its CI is wide [-0.43,+0.94]. Every FT-amplified Qwen-7B L22 band (pre + 3 FT seeds), Qwen-1.5B L27, and DeepSeek L29: difference CI straddles 0 (P(diff>0) 0.15-0.52) -> indeterminate.
results_description: The DIRECTION of the DeepSeek L23 dissociation (product > operand-magnitude) is bootstrap-robust (100% of replicates), but its effect SIZE is uncertain; all other bands cannot separate product from generic magnitude under resampling.
conclusions: Hardens a-0013 with proper uncertainty: the product-keyed-lookup signal survives operand-magnitude control only at DeepSeek L23, and only as a robust direction, not a tight effect size; the within-distribution proxy is exhausted and the GPU held-out-product finetune (e-0008/a-0010) remains the sole decisive test.
